result:
depends on cart size and pool contention from other requests
Promise.all fans out N queries simultaneously — if cart has 50 SKUs, one request eats 20 connections and queues rest
under load, concurrent orders compete for same 20 slots, tail latency spikes, timeouts cascade
sequential loop was slow but bounded: 1 connection per request
safer middle ground: bounded concurrency (p-limit or similar) at ~5 parallel per request
also check if SKU lookup is batchable into single query (WHERE sku IN (...)) — kills N+1 entirely, one connection, fastest
check current traffic: if p99 cart size is small (<5 SKUs) and RPS is low, Promise.all probably fine short-term
add a pool saturation metric before shipping if you keep Promise.all — you want to see it before customers do
